tasks.title: Implement API Authentication and RBAC for External Endpoints
tasks.description: The current task plan for sensitive API endpoints (product management, financial invoicing, customer vetting) lacks explicit authentication and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC). This task involves implementing a robust authentication mechanism (e.g., JWT, OAuth2) and RBAC policies to secure all external REST and gRPC endpoints exposed by the Equipment Catalog, RMS, Finance, and Customer Vetting services. This should ideally be enforced at an API Gateway or service-level middleware.
subtasks: Research and select an appropriate API Gateway or middleware solution for authentication and RBAC (e.g., Istio, Kong, custom middleware).
Design the authentication flow (e.g., JWT token issuance, validation).
Define RBAC roles and permissions required for admin product management, financial operations, and customer vetting.
Implement authentication and RBAC enforcement for all sensitive endpoints in services 2, 3, 4, and 5.
Integrate with an identity provider if necessary.
Conduct penetration testing against the implemented authentication and authorization mechanisms.
acceptance_criteria: All sensitive API endpoints (POST/PATCH for products, all Finance, all Customer Vetting, admin RMS) require valid authentication tokens.
Users without appropriate roles are denied access to restricted endpoints.
Authentication tokens are correctly validated and expired.
No unauthorized access is possible to any sensitive endpoint.
OWASP ZAP scan confirms no critical authentication/authorization vulnerabilities.

tasks.title: Implement Secure Inter-Service Authentication
tasks.description: The current plan lacks a concrete mechanism for inter-service authentication, leaving internal APIs vulnerable to lateral movement. This task requires implementing a zero-trust approach for all service-to-service communication between the Equipment Catalog, RMS, Finance, Customer Vetting, and Morgan AI agent services. This could involve mutual TLS (mTLS) via a service mesh or internally signed JWTs.
subtasks: Evaluate and select an inter-service authentication mechanism (e.g., mTLS with a service mesh like Istio, or internally signed JWTs).
If mTLS is chosen, configure the service mesh to enforce mTLS for all internal service communication.
If internally signed JWTs are chosen, implement a secure token issuance and validation mechanism for internal services.
Update all service clients (e.g., Morgan AI agent calling backend services, Finance calling RMS) to use the new authentication mechanism.
Ensure all internal API calls are authenticated and authorized.
acceptance_criteria: All service-to-service calls (e.g., Morgan AI to Catalog, Finance to RMS) are authenticated.
Unauthorized internal service calls are rejected.
Network traffic between services is encrypted (if mTLS is used).
Internal penetration test confirms no unauthenticated access between services.

tasks.title: Harden Morgan AI Agent Against Prompt Injection
tasks.description: The Morgan AI agent processes untrusted external input and uses it to trigger backend MCP tools, making it highly susceptible to prompt injection attacks. This task focuses on implementing robust defenses, including LLM guardrails, input sanitization, and human-in-the-loop validation for state-mutating tool calls, and ensuring the agent operates with the principle of least privilege.
subtasks: Implement LLM guardrails (e.g., using a separate model for input classification, prompt engineering techniques) to detect and mitigate malicious prompts.
Implement strict input sanitization for all user inputs before they are processed by the LLM or used in tool calls.
Introduce human-in-the-loop validation for all state-mutating MCP tool calls (e.g., sigma1_generate_quote, sigma1_create_invoice).
Review and enforce the principle of least privilege for the Morgan AI agent's service account and its access to backend services.
Implement output sanitization for any data returned by the agent to external users.
acceptance_criteria: The Morgan AI agent resists common prompt injection techniques (e.g., role-playing, instruction overriding).
Sensitive state-mutating actions triggered by the agent require explicit human confirmation.
The agent's service account has only the minimum necessary permissions.
Input and output sanitization prevents XSS or other injection attacks via the agent.
Security testing (e.g., red teaming) confirms resilience to prompt injection.

tasks.title: Implement Secure Secret Management
tasks.description: Kubernetes Secrets, by default, are only base64 encoded and not encrypted at rest, posing a risk if etcd is compromised. This task involves integrating a dedicated secret management system to securely store sensitive credentials like S3 keys, Twilio API keys, and ElevenLabs API keys, ensuring they are encrypted at rest and managed securely.
subtasks: Research and select a dedicated secret management solution (e.g., External Secrets Operator with AWS Secrets Manager/HashiCorp Vault, or Kubernetes KMS encryption at rest).
Integrate the chosen secret management solution into the Kubernetes cluster.
Migrate all existing sensitive Kubernetes Secrets (S3 credentials, Twilio API keys, ElevenLabs API keys) to the new secure secret management system.
Update applications (e.g., Morgan AI agent, infrastructure components) to retrieve secrets from the new system.
Implement secret rotation policies.
acceptance_criteria: All sensitive credentials are encrypted at rest.
Applications successfully retrieve secrets from the secure management system.
Kubernetes Secrets no longer contain raw sensitive data.
Secret rotation can be performed without service interruption.
Security audit confirms secrets are not exposed in plain text.

tasks.title: Implement Centralized Security Audit Logging
tasks.description: The current plan lacks dedicated tasks for generating and centralizing security audit logs for sensitive operations. This task requires implementing structured, centralized audit logging across all core services (Equipment Catalog, RMS, Finance, Customer Vetting) to capture critical security events, including the actor, action, resource, and timestamp for all state-mutating requests.
subtasks: Define a standardized audit log format (e.g., JSON) including fields for actor, action, resource, timestamp, and outcome.
Instrument all state-mutating API endpoints in the Equipment Catalog, RMS, Finance, and Customer Vetting services to generate audit logs.
Integrate a centralized logging solution (e.g., ELK stack, Splunk, cloud-native logging) to collect and store these audit logs.
Configure log retention policies and access controls for audit logs.
Implement alerting for critical security events detected in the audit logs.
acceptance_criteria: All state-mutating operations (e.g., product creation, invoice payment, project conversion, customer vetting run) generate a structured audit log entry.
Audit logs are successfully collected and centralized.
Audit logs contain sufficient detail (actor, action, resource, timestamp, outcome) to reconstruct security events.
Audit logs are immutable and protected from tampering.
Ability to search and filter audit logs for specific security events.
